nodes.name: 📂 Watch Ready_To_Post
nodes.notes:
Watches your Ready_To_Post folder for new .mp4 files.
Replace YOUR_READY_TO_POST_FOLDER_ID with the folder ID from the Google Drive URL.
To find it: open the folder in Drive → copy the last part of the URL.

nodes.name: ✂️ Extract File Code Name
nodes.notes:
Strips the .mp4 extension to extract the File Code Name.
Also preserves the Google Drive file ID for later download and move operations.

nodes.name: 🔍 Lookup in Tracker
nodes.notes:
Looks up the File Code Name in your Roblox Content Tracker spreadsheet
to recall the title concept, hook, script, and other metadata.
Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID with your actual spreadsheet ID.

nodes.name: 🔗 Merge File + Sheet Data
nodes.notes:
Combines the Google Drive file metadata with the spreadsheet context
so the OpenAI node has full context for metadata generation.

values.content:
You are an elite social media SEO and copywriting AI for Roblox gaming content targeting the US and Canada Gen Z / Gen Alpha audience. You will be given context about a short-form vertical video. Generate perfectly optimized metadata for cross-platform publishing.

Output strictly in this exact JSON format and NOTHING else:
{
  "yt_title": "A clickbait YouTube Shorts title, max 70 chars, include emoji, do NOT include #Shorts",
  "yt_description": "SEO-rich YouTube description, 2-3 sentences, include relevant keywords naturally",
  "yt_tags": ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3", "tag4", "tag5", "Roblox", "Shorts"],
  "tiktok_caption": "Engaging TikTok caption with Gen-Z slang, max 150 chars, 3-4 hashtags inline",
  "ig_caption": "Instagram Reels caption, slightly longer, with line breaks and 5-7 hashtags at the end",
  "fb_caption": "Facebook Reels caption, conversational tone, 2-3 hashtags",
  "hashtags": "#Roblox #RobloxShorts #Gaming + 4-5 trend-specific US hashtags"
}

nodes.name: 🧠 Generate SEO Metadata
nodes.notes:
Generates platform-specific captions, titles, descriptions, tags, and hashtags.
Lower temperature (0.7) for more consistent SEO quality.

nodes.name: 🕐 Calculate 3 PM EDT Epoch
nodes.notes:
Calculates the next 3:00 PM EDT (19:00 UTC) as both Unix epoch and ISO 8601.
If 3 PM EDT has already passed today, it schedules for tomorrow.
During US Daylight Saving Time (EDT = UTC-4), 3 PM = 19:00 UTC.
During Eastern Standard Time (EST = UTC-5), 3 PM = 20:00 UTC.
→ Adjust target.setUTCHours(20, ...) during EST months (Nov–Mar) if needed,
  or use a more robust timezone library.

nodes.name: ⬇️ Download Video
nodes.notes:
Downloads the .mp4 from Google Drive into binary property 'data'.
This is needed for YouTube Shorts (which requires binary upload)
and as a fallback if video_url isn't publicly accessible.

parameters.amount: ={{ (() => { const wait = ($('🕐 Calculate 3 PM EDT Epoch').item.json.schedule_epoch * 1000 - Date.now()) / 1000; return Math.max(wait, 0); })() }}

nodes.name: ⏳ Wait Until 3 PM EDT
nodes.notes:
TikTok has NO native scheduling API.
This Wait node pauses execution until the target 3 PM EDT timestamp,
then releases to the TikTok Publish node so it posts at the right time.
If the target time has already passed, it fires immediately (wait = 0).

nodes.name: 🎵 TikTok Publish
nodes.notes:
TikTok Content Posting API – PULL_FROM_URL.
privacy_level set to PUBLIC_TO_EVERYONE for live posting.
The Wait node before this ensures it fires at 3 PM EDT.

nodes.name: 📸 IG Reels – Create Container
nodes.notes:
Set $json.ig_user_id or the environment variable IG_USER_ID
to your Instagram Business Account numeric ID.

nodes.name: ▶️ YT Shorts – Init Upload
nodes.notes:
YouTube Data API v3 resumable upload init.
categoryId 20 = Gaming. containsSyntheticMedia = true (AI content).

nodes.name: ▶️ YT Shorts – Upload Binary
nodes.notes:
Sends the actual .mp4 binary to the resumable upload URI.
Binary property 'data' was loaded by the Download Video node.
5 minute timeout to handle large files.

nodes.name: 📘 FB Reels – Publish
nodes.notes:
Facebook Reels via Graph API.
Set $json.fb_page_id or env var FB_PAGE_ID to your Facebook Page ID.
video_state SCHEDULED uses the epoch timestamp for delayed publishing.

nodes.name: 🗃️ Move to Archive
nodes.notes:
Moves the .mp4 from Ready_To_Post → Archive to prevent duplicate triggers.
Replace YOUR_ARCHIVE_FOLDER_ID with the Archive folder ID from Google Drive.

nodes.name: 📊 Status → Scheduled
nodes.notes: Updates the row's Status column from 'Pending' to 'Scheduled'.

parameters.text:
=✅ *SCHEDULED SUCCESSFULLY*

📁 *File:* `{{ $('✂️ Extract File Code Name').item.json.file_code_name }}`
🕐 *Publish Time:* 3:00 PM EDT ({{ $('🕐 Calculate 3 PM EDT Epoch').item.json.schedule_iso }})

🎵 TikTok: ✅ Submitted
📸 IG Reels: ✅ Scheduled
▶️ YT Shorts: ✅ Scheduled
📘 FB Reels: ✅ Scheduled

📂 Video moved to Archive.
📊 Tracker updated → *Scheduled*

🛌 Go to sleep, the system has it from here!

nodes.name: 📱 Send Success Alert
nodes.notes:
Sends you a Telegram confirmation that the video has been
SEO-optimized, scheduled across all 4 platforms, archived, and tracked.
